failure_modes.text: Conjunction-style optimism can make a complex experimental rollout look safer than it is because too many dependent conditions are assumed to hold.
failure_modes.mitigation: Break complex experimental rollouts into independently testable steps; conjunctive optimism hides how many things must go right simultaneously.

failure_modes.text: Groupthink and political pressure can keep a favored hypothesis alive even when the experiment should be disproving it.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect the experiment from political pressure by pre-registering the hypothesis and the criteria for disproving it.

failure_modes.text: Experimentation theater can produce dashboards and learning language without changing the underlying decision.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when experimentation becomes theater, generating activity, dashboards, or post-hoc learning language without changing any real decision.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require that every experiment produce a decision change, not just a dashboard update; experimentation theater creates learning language without altering the plan.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
What do you think the other side doesn't get about your perspective?
premortem_questions.source_quote: “What do you think the other side doesn’t ‘get’ about your perspective?”

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Use a one-day answer so the team always has a current hypothesis and solution path guiding what facts to gather next.
Keep the experiment tied to a clear hypothesis and gather only enough facts to prove or disprove it.
heuristics.source_quote: collecting enough facts only to prove or disprove a hypothesis

heuristics.text: Separate process steps like diagnosis and design so the test does not get blurred into premature solutioning.
heuristics.source_quote: He stresses that these steps must be done one at a time and in order

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on hypothesis-linked testing, anti-theater discipline, and explicit pre-mortem challenge, so the chunk set stays close to those interventions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor product-testing sprawl because the raw file is stronger on decision-linked experimentation and anti-groupthink safeguards.
open_questions: The third premortem question is normalized from the playbook section rather than taken from a labeled mitigation prompt.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, MECE decomposition, and Feynman-style blind-spot discovery for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section explicitly names confirmation-bias, narratives, fast-thinking shortcuts, and hammer effects.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against confirmation-bias and narratives.
